findings.description: render_activity_report interpolates user-controlled display_name and action straight into HTML with no escaping, producing stored XSS in the admin dashboard.
The caller-supplied title is injected into the HTML page unescaped via PAGE_TEMPLATE.format, allowing HTML/script injection (XSS).
Docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the query uses created_at < end, silently excluding all events on the end boundary day, violating the stated contract.
limit in top_users is unvalidated; a negative value is treated by SQLite as no-limit, so it silently returns all rows instead of a bounded result.
save_report performs no error handling on open/write, so disk-full or permission errors propagate uncaught with no graceful degradation.
PAGE_TEMPLATE.format will raise KeyError/ValueError if a report value or title legitimately contains stray brace characters interpreted as format fields in edge cases, making rendering brittle.
render_activity_report builds the rows string via repeated += concatenation in a loop, which is O(n^2)-ish for very large event sets versus a list-join.
